activities_projects.title: Starch Test in Leaves
activities_projects.objective: Prove that plants produce starch during photosynthesis
steps: Boil the leaf in water for a few minutes to soften it
Place the leaf in a tube of alcohol and heat it in a water bath to remove chlorophyll
Wash the decolourised leaf in warm water
Add a few drops of iodine solution to the leaf and observe the color change
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies that a blue-black color indicates the presence of starch produced by photosynthesis
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct observation of the color change and its meaning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Observing Stomata
activities_projects.objective: View the tiny pores on a leaf surface used for gas exchange
steps: Peel off a small part of the lower epidermis of the leaf
Put the peel on a slide with a drop of water and a drop of safranin
Place a cover slip carefully to avoid air bubbles
Observe under the microscope and look for bean-shaped guard cells
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the structure of stomata and guard cells responsible for transpiration and gas exchange
activities_projects.evaluation: Sketch of the observed stomata with labeled guard cells (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Salivary Amylase Action
activities_projects.objective: Observe the effect of saliva on starch
steps: Take 1ml of starch solution in two test tubes (A and B)
Add 1ml of saliva to tube A and leave tube B as it is
Wait for 20-30 minutes
Add iodine to both tubes and observe which one turns blue-black
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that saliva contains enzymes that break down starch into simpler sugars
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of why tube A did not turn blue-black (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Carbon Dioxide and Respiration
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate that humans exhale carbon dioxide
steps: Pour some lime water into a test tube
Blow air into the lime water using the straw for a minute
Observe the change in the appearance of the lime water
Note how quickly the change occurs compared to pumping atmospheric air into it
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Confirms that carbon dioxide is a byproduct of human respiration
activities_projects.evaluation: Describing the 'milky' appearance of lime water after blowing air (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Measuring Pulse Rate
activities_projects.objective: Relate physical activity to heart rate
steps: Place two fingers on the wrist to find the pulse
Count the beats for one minute while sitting still
Run in place or do jumping jacks for 2 minutes
Count the pulse rate again immediately after exercise
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that the heart beats faster during exercise to supply more oxygen to muscles
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparison table of 'Resting Pulse' vs 'After Exercise Pulse' (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Transpiration Demonstration
activities_projects.objective: Observe water loss from plant leaves
steps: Take a small potted plant and tie a polythene bag around a leafy branch
Tie the mouth of the bag tightly
Place the plant in sunlight for 1-2 hours
Observe the inner surface of the bag
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the process of transpiration through the accumulation of water droplets
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the source of the water droplets (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Model of the Human Heart
activities_projects.objective: Learn the chambers and flow of blood in the heart
steps: Draw a simplified diagram of the four chambers of the heart
Color the sections carrying oxygenated blood red
Color the sections carrying deoxygenated blood blue
Draw arrows to show the path of blood from the lungs to the body
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the separation of oxygenated and deoxygenated blood in a 4-chambered heart
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct labeling of atria, ventricles, and main valves (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Testing for CO2 in Plants
activities_projects.objective: Observe if plants release CO2 during the night
materials: Bent glass tube and beaker of water
steps: Place germinating seeds in a flask and a small tube of KOH inside to absorb CO2
Seal the flask with a cork and an airtight bent tube dipping into water
Wait for a few hours
Observe the rise in the water level in the tube
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates that germinating seeds perform respiration and use oxygen, creating a vacuum
activities_projects.evaluation: Reasoning for the rise in the water level (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mapping the Digestive System
activities_projects.objective: Identify the organs involved in human nutrition
steps: List the organs of the Alimentary Canal in order (Mouth to Anus)
Write the main function of the Stomach, Small Intestine, and Large Intestine
Identify where the final digestion of food takes place
Name the largest gland in the body (Liver)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Summarizes the path and process of food digestion in humans
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct sequential list of organs and their basic functions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Excretion in Plants
materials: Samples of gum, resin, or dried leaves
steps: Go to a garden and look for sticky substances (gums/resins) on tree trunks
Observe old yellow leaves falling from a tree
Research why plants shed their leaves and bark
List three ways plants get rid of waste
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that plants use different methods (shedding, storage in vacuoles, resins) for excretion
activities_projects.evaluation: Listing three distinct methods of plant excretion (10 marks)
